narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense variant Q1396R (p.Gln1396Arg) is a single‑nucleotide change (c.4187A>G) located in exon 11 of the BRCA2 gene. It has been evaluated in the context of hereditary breast cancer risk.

In a cohort of Mauritanian breast‑cancer patients, the Q1396R allele was identified in one individual and was classified as benign by the authors, who reported it among a list of other BRCA2 variants also deemed non‑pathogenic [PMC9301826].

No functional assays, segregation analyses, or computational predictions were provided in the extracted evidence, and no population frequency data were presented beyond the single carrier reported in this study.

Taken together, the only available evidence classifies BRCA2 Q1396R as a benign variant, and there are no conflicting reports of pathogenicity in the supplied literature.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study screened Mauritanian breast cancer patients and reported the BRCA2 Q1396R (p.Gln1396Arg) variant as benign in their cohort data.
article_mentions.title: Screening of BRCA1/2 variants in Mauritanian breast cancer patients
